name: telehealth_psychiatry_recording_consent
premise: A telehealth platform introduces automatic session recording to improve clinician training and defend against malpractice claims. Psychiatrist Dr. Sara Ahmed finds that patients with trauma histories are less willing to disclose when recorded, and one patient threatens to stop care. The platform offers an opt-out, but it is buried in settings and defaults to on. If Sara refuses to use recordings, she may lose access to the platform and abandon patients in rural areas; if she complies, she may erode therapeutic trust and create data breach risk. A recent hack of a similar platform is in the news. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Mental Health. The dilemma is widely framed as a autonomy vs beneficence tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay.

agents.name: Dr. Sara Ahmed
agents.identity: I am a psychiatrist responsible for maintaining therapeutic trust when platform policies change the privacy expectations of care.
agents.goal: I want patients to be safe and honest in treatment without coercing them into recordings they do not understand or want.

agents.name: Product VP Ben Wallace
agents.identity: I am the product executive responsible for risk management and quality improvement features that require consistent data collection.
agents.goal: I want recordings enabled by default to improve training and defense, because selective adoption undermines safety and accountability.

choices: I will require explicit informed consent for recordings and disable defaults for my patients, accepting friction to preserve trust and reduce breach harm.
I will use default recording with clear notices and rely on the opt-out, accepting reduced disclosure to maintain platform access and standardization.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Consent and access are in tension for vulnerable patients. Dr. Sara Ahmed must choose between requiring explicit recording consent or using default recording to preserve platform access and standardization. What does Dr. Sara Ahmed do?
